---
title: "Welcome"
toc: false
image: images/IMG_9871.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 20em
  links: 
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sarah-kopitz-7942623a2/
    - icon: envelope-at
      text: Email
      href: mailto:snkopitz@gmail.com
---


## About me

I’m Sarah Kopitz, originally from Brandon, Manitoba. I moved to Winnipeg to complete my BSc in Environmental Science, specializing in Forest Ecology, at the University of Winnipeg, and later moved to Vancouver to complete the Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management program at the University of British Columbia.

I am proudly Anishinaabe and German. Indigenous rights, land, and the respectful representation of knowledge are important to me and shape how I approach environmental work.

I’m especially interested in GIS, remote sensing, and working with data to better understand environmental issues. Geomatics stands out to me because it combines technical problem solving with creativity, whether I’m building workflows, analyzing information, or communicating results through maps and visuals.

My interests are rooted in a strong connection to the environment, especially forests, water, wildlife, and the relationships between people and the land.
